stories.title: Tensions rise in two ceasefires in the Middle East. And, the Navy secretary ousted
stories.description: Tensions are rising in the Middle East as shaky ceasefire agreements between the U.S., Israel and Iran, and Lebanon and Israel, are tested. And, the Secretary of the Navy is out of the role.

stories.title: U.S. seizes another oil tanker as peace talks with Iran still in limbo
stories.description: The seizure comes after President Trump told Fox News Wednesday that there was "no time pressure" on the ceasefire with Iran, which he has extended indefinitely.

stories.title: Iranians are leaving the country just to access the internet
stories.description: Iran has cut off its access to the global internet. To find an internet connection, some Iranians are traveling across the border with Turkey — even just to make video calls and then go back home.

stories.title: Lebanon accuses Israel of targeting journalist killed in air strike
stories.description: Lebanon's prime minister accused Israel of war crimes after IDF attacks on Red Cross vehicles also stopped rescuers from reaching the site.

stories.title: Iran says Strait of Hormuz cannot be opened due to ceasefire breaches
stories.description: Iran's chief negotiator says "violations" by the US and Israel make it impossible to open the strait.

stories.title: US and Iran in blockade standoff as Pakistan pushes for talks
stories.description: The mood in the Strait of Hormuz remains combustible despite Trump's ceasefire extension.

stories.title: Tracking ships under fire in the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Iran's top negotiator says it's "not possible to reopen" the strait due to "blatent violations" of US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.title: US and Iran 'locked in war of words' says Lyse Doucet
stories.description: The US confirmed on Tuesday night that the Vice-President's trip to Pakistan for a second round of peace talks has been cancelled.

stories.title: Iran says it has seized two ships in Strait of Hormuz after vessels attacked
stories.description: One of the cargo ships was 'heavily damaged' by Iranian forces, a British maritime agency reported on Wednesday.

stories.title: Trump buys time for Iran deal after frantic day of diplomacy
stories.description: The US president's decision marked the second time in as many weeks he has backed off a threat to escalate the war, buying more time.

stories.title: Iran sees mass redundancies from war with US and Israel
stories.description: The impact is being felt by manufacturers, retailers and the digital sector, amid warnings it could get worse if the war resumes.

stories.title: Israeli soldiers punished over vandalism of Jesus statue in Lebanon
stories.description: Two soldiers will be removed from combat duty and receive 30 days of military detention, Israel's military says.

stories.title: Why and how is US blockading Iranian ports in Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: Donald Trump says that the US is blockading the Strait of Hormuz. What does this mean in practice?

stories.title: Washington uses Iraq's own oil money to bend Baghdad to its will
stories.description: The Trump administration has blocked a shipment of nearly $500 million in cash to Iraq, pressing Baghdad to dismantle powerful Iranian-backed armed groups. Analysts say the blockage risks destabilising an already fragile economy.

stories.title: Second French peacekeeper dies in Lebanon after ambush blamed on Hezbollah
stories.description: A second French soldier died on Wednesday from wounds suffered in a weekend ambush against UN peacekeepers in Lebanon blamed on Hezbollah, President Emmanuel Macron said. A first soldier, staff sergeant Florian Montorio, was shot dead in the Saturday ambush, for which Hezbollah h...

stories.title: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon kill Lebanese journalist, wound another
stories.description: Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon on Wednesday killed Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil and wounded a photographer accompanying her, according to a senior Lebanese military official and Khalil's employer, the Al-Akhbar newspaper. The death of Khalil, 43, brought the death toll on...

stories.title: New round of talks in Washington: Lebanon, Israel to discuss truce extension
stories.description: Lebanese ​and Israeli envoys will meet for the second time in two weeks in Washington on Thursday, building on nascent contacts between the states, with Lebanon hoping for the extension of a shaky ceasefire between Hezbollah and Israel. The 10-day, US-mediated ceasefire is set to...

stories.title: Iran tightens control of Hormuz after US calls off renewed attacks
stories.description: Iran seized two ships in the Strait of Hormuz as it tightened its grip on ​the strategic waterway after US President Donald Trump announced he was indefinitely calling off attacks, with no sign of peace talks restarting. The status of a two-week-old ceasefire, due to have expired...

stories.title: EU 'must' shift to renewables to end fossil fuel 'blackmail from war-driving authoritarian regimes'
stories.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Hannah Neumann, German MEP, Greens/EFA and Chair of EU Parliament's Delegation for Iran. She offers analysis on the growing geopolitical instability surrounding Iran, energy security, and the EU’s strategic position in an increasingly fragmen...

stories.title: Protester hurls red fluid at Reza Pahlavi during Germany visit
stories.description: The exiled former crown prince of Iran Reza Pahlavi was splashed with red fluid by an activist during a visit to Berlin.

stories.title: How has Hezbollah shifted its battle tactics?
stories.description: Hezbollah’s change of weapons and tactics

stories.title: The economic winners & losers in the US-Israel war on Iran
stories.description: The economic winners & losers in the US-Israel war on Iran

stories.title: Indian physicists voice solidarity with Iran, Palestine, Lebanon academics
stories.description: In a statement, prominent string theorists condemn the attacks on universities across the Middle Eastern countries.

stories.title: How Iran raised Hormuz stakes by capturing ships
stories.description: After the US and Israel launched a war on Iran on February 28, Tehran blocked the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Iran war: How big is the global energy shock?
stories.description: More than 500 million barrels of oil disrupted in just weeks, reshaping global energy flows.

stories.title: Africa’s abandoned workers in Lebanon
stories.description: African migrant workers in Lebanon are caught in a war.

stories.title: Lufthansa cuts 20,000 flights as Iran war causes jet fuel shortage
stories.description: The German airline says 20,000 short-haul flights would be removed from its ⁠schedule between now and October.

stories.title: AI startup Cursor with Pakistani co-founder set for $60bn deal with SpaceX
stories.description: Cursor, an AI code-generation startup co-founded by Pakistani-born Sualeh Asif, has secured a $60 billion acquisition deal with Elon Musk’s SpaceX, according to multiple reports on Thursday. Along with OpenAI and Anthropic, Cursor is one of several Silicon Valley startups that ha...

stories.title: Israeli airstrike kills journalist in south Lebanon: civil defence
stories.description: An Israeli airstrike killed a Lebanese journalist and wounded another on Wednesday while they were working near the border with Israel, according to their employer and rescuers. The civil defence agency’s press office said rescuers were “able to recover the body of martyred journ...

stories.title: Lebanon, Israel to meet today as truce hangs by a thread
stories.description: • Envoys’ meeting in Washington aims to extend ceasefire, halt Israel’s demolitions• Beirut sees extension as first step to talks on forces’ withdrawal, return of detainees• Israeli strikes kill five in Lebanon, including a journalist WASHINGTON: Lebanese and Israeli envoys are s...

stories.title: US optimistic about Iran talks ‘this week’
stories.description: • Trump says resumption of negotiations in Islamabad possible within ‘36 to 72 hours’; appreciates Tehran for stopping ‘execution’ of detained women• Pezeshkian welcomes dialogue, cites ‘breach of commitments, blockade and threats’ as main obstacles• Trump denies media reports ab...

stories.title: Iran appreciates Pakistan’s efforts to mediate between Tehran and Washington
stories.description: Iran’s foreign ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei said on Wednesday that the Islamic republic appreciated Pakistan’s efforts to end the Middle East war, without commenting on a ceasefire extension announced by the United States. He expressed these views while answering reporters’ ...

stories.title: Indonesia denounces Israel ‘propaganda’ banner over Gaza hospital ruins
stories.description: Indonesia on Wednesday accused Israel of flying a “propaganda” banner over the ruins of a hospital that had been built in Gaza with Indonesian funding. The Indonesia Hospital, in the north of the Gaza Strip near the fortified border with Israel, was opened in late 2015 after Indo...

stories.title: U.S. military says it seizes another oil tanker associated with Iran
stories.description: The seizure comes a day after Iran attacked three cargo ships in the strait, capturing two of them, in a move that intensified its assault on shipping in the key waterway through which 20% of the world's traded oil passes in peacetime.

stories.title: Reza Pahlavi splattered with red liquid in Berlin
stories.description: The son of Iran’s ousted shah called on Europeans to do more to support Iranian people; alleged perpetrator detained

stories.title: Israel-Iran LIVE: U.S. military says it seized another oil tanker associated with Iran
stories.description: Iran’s vow not to reopen the Strait of Hormuz so long as a U.S. naval blockade remained in place

stories.title: Pakistan Minister, U.S. envoy discuss efforts for 2nd round of U.S.-Iran talks
stories.description: The meeting comes amid stepped-up diplomatic engagements aimed at bringing Tehran and Washington to the negotiating table, possibly by the weekend

stories.title: Lebanon to seek ceasefire extension in U.S.-hosted talks with Israel
stories.description: The U.S.-mediated ceasefire has yielded a significant reduction in violence, but attacks have continued in southern Lebanon, where Israeli troops have seized a self-declared buffer zone

stories.title: Watch: U.S. extended sanctions waiver on Russian oil purchase after request from over 10 countries
